caveats: This is a one-attempt baseline benchmark, not a replicated capability estimate or a memory-treatment result.
Missing usage or cost remains unavailable; it is never treated as zero.
Raw Agent conversations and tool content remain in authenticated Weave.
findings: Kimi resolved 8 of 32 tasks overall; Hermes resolved 3, Codex 2, OpenClaw 2, and Claude Code 1.
The same model and task set produced distinct harness outcomes, supporting harness as an experimental factor rather than presentation state.
hypothesis: Harnesses will produce materially different resolution and efficiency profiles even when they share one fixed model and task set.

cohorts.label: Kimi K2.7 Code across four harnesses

question: How much does the Agent harness change Kimi K2.7 Code's ability to resolve structurally hard software repairs when model, tasks, prompt, and runtime are fixed?
summary: A complete no-context capability matrix across four Agent harnesses and eight locked hard SWE-bench discovery tasks.
task_selection: The locked discovery partition requires production and test changes, two to eight touched files, repository diversity, and either lexical mismatch or cross-directory production work.
title: Kimi K2.7 Code × harness baseline
why_it_matters: This is the first complete denominator for separating model capability from harness behavior before repository memory is introduced.
